import re

# Files to clean console.log/warn (keep console.error for real error handling)
files = [
    'app/(tabs)/(home)/home.tsx',
    'app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx',
    'app/(tabs)/chat.tsx',
    'app/(tabs)/invest/index.tsx',
    'app/videos.tsx',
    'app/landing.tsx',
    'app/jv-invest.tsx',
    'components/InvestorFirstFeed.tsx',
]

for fpath in files:
    with open(fpath, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
    # Remove console.log lines (entire line including the semicolon)
    # Match: console.log(...); or console.warn(...); on a single line
    new_content = re.sub(r'\s*console\.(log|warn)\([^;]+;\s*\n', '\n', content)
    if new_content != content:
        with open(fpath, 'w') as f:
            f.write(new_content)
        removed = content.count('console.log') + content.count('console.warn') - new_content.count('console.log') - new_content.count('console.warn')
        print(f'{fpath}: removed {removed} console.log/warn statements')
    else:
        print(f'{fpath}: no changes (pattern not matched on single lines)')
"
